case_title: RANDALL M BAHNUIK VS. DEPARTMENT OF MOTOR VEHICLES, AN AGENCY OF THE
cause_of_action: WRITS OF MANDATE OR PROH., CERTI., ETC./ADMIN. AGEN

docket_entries.description: OPPOSITION TO PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE FILED BY RESPONDENT DEPARTMENT OF MOTOR VEHICLES, AN AGENCY OF THE STATE OF CALIFORNIA

PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE/ PROHIBITION/ CERTIFICATION FILED BY PETITIONER BAHNUIK, RANDALL M AS TO RESPONDENT DEPARTMENT OF MOTOR VEHICLES, AN AGENCY OF THE STATE OF CALIFORNIA JUDICIAL COUNCIL CIVIL CASE COVER SHEET FILED
ANSWER TO PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE/ PROHIBITION/ CERTIFICATION FILED BY RESPONDENT DEPARTMENT OF MOTOR VEHICLES, AN AGENCY OF THE STATE OF CALIFORNIA

LAW AND MOTION 302, PETITIONER RANDALL BAHNUIK'S NOTICE OF HEARING RE: WRIT OF MANDATE. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: PETITIONER'S REQUEST FOR WRIT OF MANDATE IS DENIED. PETITIONER HAS FAILED TO MEET ITS BURDEN OF SHOWING THAT THE DMV HEARING OFFICER'S DECISION WAS CONTRARY TO THE WEIGHT OF THE EVIDENCE. A REVIEW OF THE ADMINISTRATIVE RECORD SHOWS THAT THERE WAS MORE THAN SUFFICIENT EVIDENCE TO SUPPORT THE DMV HEARING OFFICER'S FINDINGS AND CONCLUSION. ORDER AND JUDGMENT SIGNED IN OPEN COURT.
NOTICE OF ENTRY OF JUDGMENT FILED BY RESPONDENT DEPARTMENT OF MOTOR VEHICLES, AN AGENCY OF THE STATE OF CALIFORNIA
MINI-MINUTES FOR AUG-10-2011 9:30 AM FOR DEPT 302

calendar.matters:
Notice Of Hearing Re: Writ Of Mandate
Off Calendar Aug-10-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PETITIONER RANDALL BAHNUIK'S NOTICE OF HEARING RE: WRIT OF MANDATE.
Appearances: Bonnie Chen of the Office of the Attorney General (510.622.2113) for respondent and opposing party; Adam G. Gasner (782.6000) for petitioner and moving party.
Argued and the court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Petitioner's request for writ of mandate is denied. Petitioner has failed to meet its burden of showing that the DMV hearing officer's decision was contrary to the weight of the evidence. A review of the administrative record shows that there was more than sufficient evidence to support the DMV hearing officer's findings and conclusion. Order and judgment signed in open court. (302/LMG) Judge: Loretta M. Giorgi, Reporter: Karla Ellis-Davis, CSR #12998
